2605.18229.title: Are Sparse Autoencoder Benchmarks Reliable?
2605.18229.paper_summary: Sparse autoencoders (SAEs) are a core interpretability tool for large language models, and progress on SAE architectures depends on benchmarks that reliably distinguish better SAEs from worse ones. We audit the SAE quality metrics in SAEBench, the de-facto standard SAE evaluation suite, through three complementary lenses: reseed noise on a fixed SAE, ground-truth correlation on synthetic SAEs, and discriminability across training trajectories. We find that two of these metrics, Targeted Probe Perturbation (TPP) and Spurious Correlation Removal (SCR), fail multiple lenses at their canonical settings and should not be used to evaluate SAEs. The other metrics show higher reseed noise and lower discriminability than the field assumes. The sae-probes variant of $k$-sparse probing is the most reliable metric we tested, but even sae-probes struggles to separate variants of the same SAE architecture. Our results show the field needs better SAE benchmarks.
2605.18229.paper_summary_zh: 稀疏自編碼器（SAEs）是大型語言模型的一個核心可解釋性工具，而SAE架構的進展依賴於能夠可靠區分優劣SAE的基準。我們通過三個互補的視角審核SAEBench中的SAE質量指標，這是事實上標準的SAE評估套件：在固定SAE上的重播噪聲、合成SAE上的真實相關性，以及訓練軌跡中的可區分性。我們發現這些指標中的兩個，目標探測擾動（TPP）和虛假相關性去除（SCR），在其典型設置下在多個視角中失效，應該不被用來評估SAE。其他指標顯示出比該領域假設的更高的重播噪聲和更低的可區分性。$k$-稀疏探測的sae-probes變體是我們測試過的最可靠指標，但即使是sae-probes也難以區分相同SAE架構的變體。我們的結果顯示該領域需要更好的SAE基準。
2605.18229.author: David Chanin et.al.
